speaker: Aria
text: The team chose Flask as the backend framework for the CareerPilot app because everyone already knew it.

speaker: Kabir
text: Deployment was planned on Railway, with Supabase used only for authentication.

speaker: Mira
text: The team found that async endpoints and OpenAPI docs would be important for the interview simulator.

speaker: Aria
text: The backend framework decision was changed from Flask to FastAPI to support async routes and automatic OpenAPI documentation.

speaker: Kabir
text: Railway remained the deployment target after the framework change.

speaker: Mira
text: The team decided not to use Django because the current API surface is small and the ORM is not needed.

speaker: Nisha
text: The roadmap feature will initially support four target roles: AI Engineer, ML Engineer, Data Scientist, and Applied Scientist.

speaker: Dev
text: The first plan was to generate roadmaps using only the uploaded resume.

speaker: Nisha
text: Market trend data was added as a required input because the roadmap should reflect current role demand.

speaker: Omar
text: The team decided that LinkedIn profile scraping would not be included in v1 because of privacy and reliability risks.

speaker: Dev
text: The v1 roadmap output must include weekly milestones, skill gaps, project suggestions, and interview preparation tasks.

speaker: Nisha
text: The resume-only generation plan is outdated; the accepted design uses resume plus selected role plus market trends.

speaker: Leena
text: The team planned to integrate Razorpay before deployment using ngrok for local webhook testing.

speaker: Ravi
text: The webhook endpoint will be /api/billing/webhook and should listen for payment.captured events.

speaker: Leena
text: Because the site is not deployed yet, the team decided to postpone live Razorpay verification.

speaker: Ravi
text: The team decided to use test mode now and complete production payment testing after deployment.

speaker: Maya
text: The ngrok setup was dropped for the current sprint to avoid unnecessary complexity.

speaker: Leena
text: The final sprint scope is to store Razorpay environment variables and leave full webhook testing for post-deployment.

speaker: Ishan
text: The first experiment plan was to fine-tune a 7B model for all memory extraction tasks.

speaker: Sara
text: The group confirmed that no paid APIs should be required for the benchmark prototype.

speaker: Ishan
text: Because local compute is CPU-only, the 7B fine-tuning plan is not feasible for v0.

speaker: Sara
text: The new plan is to use prompt-based extraction with free-tier hosted LLMs and open-source embeddings.

speaker: Meera
text: For reproducibility, every baseline must use the same embedding model, chunk size, LLM, and question set.

speaker: Ishan
text: Fine-tuning can be reconsidered only after the v0 benchmark and baseline results are complete.

speaker: Tara
text: The initial web voice plan used the browser SpeechSynthesis API with whatever default voice the browser selected.

speaker: Kunal
text: The platform includes male and female interviewer personas across companies.

speaker: Tara
text: The voice assignment logic was updated: male interviewer personas should use available male voices, and female personas should use available female voices.

speaker: Kunal
text: If the browser does not expose a matching gendered voice, the app should fall back to the clearest available English voice.

speaker: Tara
text: Hosted TTS APIs are not included in v1 because the project should minimize recurring costs.

speaker: Kunal
text: Users should be able to replay the spoken question, but voice cloning is explicitly out of scope.

speaker: Priya
text: The first tagging plan tried to balance every company tag equally across all question banks.

speaker: Arjun
text: The company field is stored as an array, not as a plain text string.

speaker: Priya
text: The tagging rule changed: company tags should be selected based on realistic topic-company fit, not forced balancing.

speaker: Arjun
text: Popular companies like MAANG, Microsoft, and OpenAI should still appear where relevant, but not in every bank.

speaker: Priya
text: For SQL queries against the company field, array-aware operators must be used instead of equality against a string.

speaker: Arjun
text: The team rejected blind company-count balancing because it produces unrealistic interview tags.

speaker: Sameer
text: The first deployment idea used a Python backend on a traditional VM with manual process management.

speaker: Lina
text: The app depends mainly on Supabase for storage and auth, and external model APIs for generation.

speaker: Sameer
text: Cloudflare became the preferred deployment target because the app is mostly frontend/API orchestration and needs low cost.

speaker: Lina
text: The team decided not to host large models directly in the first 2-3 months.

speaker: Sameer
text: If Python-only services are required later, they will be isolated behind separate serverless or container endpoints.

speaker: Lina
text: The VM deployment plan is no longer preferred because it increases maintenance effort before product validation.

speaker: Yash
text: The first paper plan evaluated only one synthetic document and one RAG baseline.

speaker: Rhea
text: The target venue requires strong baselines and controlled comparisons.

speaker: Yash
text: The experiment plan was expanded to compare vanilla RAG, rerank RAG, contextual retrieval, agentic RAG, GraphRAG, and living memory.

speaker: Rhea
text: Every system must answer with final answer, evidence IDs, retrieved context IDs, latency, and token count.

speaker: Yash
text: The one-baseline plan was rejected because it would not support a strong publication claim.

speaker: Rhea
text: Ablations must include no temporal validity, no contradiction handling, and no provenance reranking.

speaker: User
text: I will revise probability first, then move to linear algebra next week.

speaker: User
text: The ML interview is now scheduled earlier than expected, so I need to prioritize ML fundamentals.

speaker: Assistant
text: The updated study order is ML fundamentals first, then probability, then linear algebra.

speaker: User
text: I only have 90 minutes per day on weekdays.

speaker: User
text: Skip deep reinforcement learning for now because it is unlikely for this interview.

speaker: Assistant
text: The earlier probability-first plan is superseded by the ML-fundamentals-first plan.

speaker: Neel
text: The ETL pipeline was initially assigned to the backend team.

speaker: Asha
text: The pipeline has three stages: extract from Kaggle, transform column names, and load into Supabase.

speaker: Neel
text: Ownership changed because the data team needs to validate schema and quality before loading.

speaker: Asha
text: The data team is now the DRI for ETL, while backend owns only the API that reads from Supabase.

speaker: Neel
text: The backend team should not modify transform logic without data team review.

speaker: Asha
text: The old backend-owned ETL assignment is no longer valid.

speaker: Farah
text: The first monitoring plan checked only model accuracy once per month.

speaker: Leo
text: The team wants to detect feature drift before accuracy drops significantly.

speaker: Farah
text: The monitoring plan was updated to compute PSI weekly for key numerical and categorical features.

speaker: Leo
text: Retraining should be triggered if PSI crosses the alert threshold for two consecutive checks.

speaker: Farah
text: The monthly-accuracy-only monitoring plan is obsolete.

speaker: Leo
text: The v0 dashboard should show drift status, last check time, and whether retraining is recommended.

speaker: Hema
text: The first moderation rule blocked every negative comment.

speaker: Rohan
text: The classifier outputs toxicity, sentiment, and spam labels.

speaker: Hema
text: The moderation rule was revised: toxic or spam comments should be blocked, but negative non-toxic comments should be allowed with warning.

speaker: Rohan
text: Neutral comments should be allowed unless they are toxic or spam.

speaker: Hema
text: The block-all-negative rule was rejected because it suppresses valid criticism.

speaker: Rohan
text: The UI must display the moderation decision as allow, warn, or block.

speaker: Milan
text: The initial architecture review process required only the feature team's engineering manager approval.

speaker: Reva
text: The new caching layer may affect authentication, billing, and analytics workflows.

speaker: Milan
text: Because the change is far-reaching, review now requires the feature EM, security representative, and platform owner.

speaker: Reva
text: A rollout plan with monitoring metrics is required before release.

speaker: Milan
text: The EM-only approval process is no longer sufficient for this caching change.

speaker: Reva
text: The team decided not to release the caching layer behind a permanent dark launch; feature flags will be temporary.

speaker: Anika
text: The first escalation workflow routed all customer bugs through the normal backlog.

speaker: Jules
text: A critical enterprise customer reported that the bug blocks their month-end reporting process.

speaker: Anika
text: The workflow changed: critical business-impact bugs get priority::1 and must be scheduled within one business day after required approvals.

speaker: Jules
text: Daily progress updates must be posted in the escalated customer Slack channel.

speaker: Anika
text: The normal-backlog-only route is no longer valid for approved critical escalations.

speaker: Jules
text: Feature requests still require Product DRI approval even if they come from the escalated account.

speaker: User
text: For the demo, prepare a Streamlit dashboard with three charts.

speaker: User
text: The demo audience changed from internal teammates to external investors.

speaker: User
text: Because investors care about traction, replace one technical chart with a user growth chart.

speaker: Assistant
text: The current demo dashboard should include revenue trend, user growth, and model reliability charts.

speaker: User
text: Do not include raw database latency unless someone asks during Q&A.

speaker: Assistant
text: The original three-technical-charts plan is superseded by the investor-focused chart plan.

speaker: Vikram
text: The initial security review covered only authentication endpoints.

speaker: Nora
text: The new billing feature touches authentication, invoices, payment webhooks, and user entitlements.

speaker: Vikram
text: Security review scope was expanded to include payment webhooks and entitlement changes.

speaker: Nora
text: Invoice display changes require privacy review but not a full security threat model.

speaker: Vikram
text: The auth-only review scope is obsolete for the billing release.

speaker: Nora
text: The release cannot ship until webhook signature verification is tested.

speaker: Amit
text: The first benchmark plan used only real company handbooks.

speaker: Zoya
text: Manual annotation of long real documents is too slow for the current deadline.

speaker: Amit
text: The dataset plan changed to synthetic scenario streams with gold memory atoms and evidence IDs.

speaker: Zoya
text: Real documents may still be used later as an external validation set.

speaker: Amit
text: Every synthetic scenario must include at least one update, one superseded fact, and one abstention question.

speaker: Zoya
text: The real-handbook-only plan is dropped for v0 because it creates too much manual work.

speaker: Eli
text: The initial agent policy allowed the coding agent to run shell commands automatically after planning.

speaker: Mina
text: Some shell commands can modify files or delete generated artifacts.

speaker: Eli
text: The policy changed: read-only commands may run automatically, but write/delete commands require user approval.

speaker: Mina
text: Network calls are disabled unless the task explicitly requires external data.

speaker: Eli
text: The unrestricted automatic shell command policy is no longer valid.

speaker: Mina
text: The agent must log each tool call with timestamp, command category, and approval status.
